title: Checkpoints, Write-Ahead State, Crash Consistency, and Resume · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A university documentation platform and independent departmental publishers. They need bounded sitemap and link discovery, trap containment, origin fairness, Promise backpressure, cancellation, crash-consistent checkpoints, and restart evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Fair Crash-Resumable Documentation Crawler with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Bound index depth, files, URLs, bytes, redirects, duplicate entries, duration, and cycles before traversal.
Atomically reserve admitted URL identity when scheduling so concurrent discovery cannot duplicate effects.
Budget distinct keys, values, combinations, orderings, and per-template pages with stakeholder-approved exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Index Bounds
steps.instruction:
project case — A project TypeScript team handles case 9221 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound index depth, files, URLs, bytes, redirects, duplicate entries, duration, and cycles before traversal.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Index Bounds.
